## Constitution & Rights

1. Germany's constitution is called the Grundgesetz.

    > Keywords: Grundgesetz, Verfassung

    > Questions: 6,11

1. Article 1 of the Grundgesetz guarantees the inviolability of human dignity.

    > Keywords: Artikel 1, Menschenwürde

    > Questions: 18

1. Meinungsfreiheit is a Grundrecht and lets people openly express opinions, including in reader letters and criticism of government.

    > Keywords: Meinungsfreiheit, Grundrecht, Leserbriefe

    > Questions: 1,4,14

1. Pressefreiheit is a Grundrecht and cannot be abolished by a Bundestag party.

    > Keywords: Pressefreiheit, Grundrecht, Bundestag

    > Questions: 12

1. Meinungsfreiheit is limited when false claims about individual persons are publicly spread.

    > Keywords: Meinungsfreiheit, Einschränkung, falsche Behauptungen

    > Questions: 16

1. Glaubens- und Gewissensfreiheit is a constitutional Grundrecht.

    > Keywords: Glaubensfreiheit, Gewissensfreiheit, Grundrecht

    > Questions: 7

1. Asyl is the Grundrecht in Germany that applies only to foreigners.

    > Keywords: Asyl, Ausländerinnen, Ausländer, Grundrecht

    > Questions: 9

1. Freizügigkeit means people may choose their own place of residence.

    > Keywords: Freizügigkeit, Wohnort

    > Questions: 19

1. The Grundgesetz forbids Zwangsarbeit, while Geldstrafe is compatible with it.

    > Keywords: Zwangsarbeit, Geldstrafe, Grundgesetz

    > Questions: 10,15

1. Germany's equality principle forbids state discrimination and unequal treatment, including disability discrimination.

    > Keywords: Gleichbehandlung, Ungleichbehandlung, Behinderung

    > Questions: 17,262

1. The Grundgesetz does not promise that everyone should have the same amount of money.

    > Keywords: Grundgesetz, Gleichheit, Geld

    > Questions: 8

1. In Germany, state and religious communities are separate.

    > Keywords: Staat, Religion, Trennung

    > Questions: 33

1. German society follows religious tolerance: each person may believe what they want.

    > Keywords: religiöse Toleranz, Glauben

    > Questions: 292

1. Briefgeheimnis means intentionally opening a letter addressed to someone else violates protected correspondence.

    > Keywords: Briefgeheimnis, Post, Rechte

    > Questions: 274

## Government & Federalism

1. Germany is a republic, not a monarchy.

    > Keywords: Republik, Monarchie, Staatsform

    > Questions: 22,34

1. Germany is a democratic, social Bundesstaat.

    > Keywords: demokratisch, sozial, Bundesstaat

    > Questions: 26,27,38

1. Volkssouveränität means all state power comes from the people.

    > Keywords: Volkssouveränität, Staatsgewalt, Volk

    > Questions: 52,61

1. In a Rechtsstaat, residents and the state must obey the laws.

    > Keywords: Rechtsstaat, Gesetze, Staat

    > Questions: 3,53

1. Gewaltenteilung separates Legislative, Exekutive, and Judikative; Presse and Direktive are not state powers.

    > Keywords: Gewaltenteilung, Legislative, Exekutive, Judikative

    > Questions: 32,54,60

1. Bundestag and Bundesrat belong to the Legislative.

    > Keywords: Bundestag, Bundesrat, Legislative

    > Questions: 60

1. Courts and judges belong to the Judikative, the rechtsprechende Gewalt.

    > Keywords: Judikative, Richter, rechtsprechende Gewalt

    > Questions: 143,144,145

1. Germany has 16 Bundesländer, each with its own government.

    > Keywords: Bundesländer, Regierung, Föderalismus

    > Questions: 24,39

1. The Federal Republic is organized into Bund, Länder, and Kommunen, with Gemeinden as the lowest political level.

    > Keywords: Bund, Länder, Kommunen, Gemeinden

    > Questions: 64,69

1. School policy is mainly decided by the Bundesländer, and the state controls schools because the Grundgesetz assigns it that task.

    > Keywords: Schulpolitik, Bundesländer, Schulwesen

    > Questions: 49,67,68

1. The Bundestag is the parliament for all of Germany, elected by the eligible people.

    > Keywords: Bundestag, Parlament, wahlberechtigtes Volk

    > Questions: 28,74

1. Parliament decides new laws in Germany.

    > Keywords: Parlament, Gesetzgebung, Gesetz

    > Questions: 42

1. The Bundestag does not form the Bundeskabinett.

    > Keywords: Bundestag, Bundeskabinett, Aufgaben

    > Questions: 65

1. The Bundesrat is formed by government representatives of the Bundesländer and lets the Länder participate in federal legislation.

    > Keywords: Bundesrat, Bundesländer, Gesetzgebung

    > Questions: 85,90

1. A state government change can affect federal politics if it changes the Bundesrat majority.

    > Keywords: Bundesrat, Mehrheit, Bundespolitik

    > Questions: 91

1. The Bundespräsident is Germany's Staatsoberhaupt and mainly represents the country.

    > Keywords: Bundespräsident, Staatsoberhaupt, Repräsentation

    > Questions: 84,87

1. The Bundesversammlung elects the Bundespräsident; citizens do not directly elect that office.

    > Keywords: Bundesversammlung, Bundespräsident, direkte Wahl

    > Questions: 44,86

1. The Bundespräsident appoints federal ministers and proposes the Chancellor for election.

    > Keywords: Bundespräsident, Minister, Kanzlerwahl

    > Questions: 58,70

1. The Bundestag elects the Bundeskanzlerin/den Bundeskanzler, who leads the Bundeskabinett.

    > Keywords: Bundestag, Bundeskanzler, Bundeskabinett

    > Questions: 81,82,83

1. The Chancellor works most often in Berlin because the Bundeskanzleramt and Bundestag are there.

    > Keywords: Berlin, Bundeskanzleramt, Bundestag

    > Questions: 71

## Elections & Parties

1. Wahlen in Germany are free, equal, and secret, and no voter may be forced or disadvantaged for their vote.

    > Keywords: Wahlen, frei, gleich, geheim

    > Questions: 5,112,122

1. Voting in democratic elections is a right, and aktives Wahlrecht means a person can vote.

    > Keywords: aktives Wahlrecht, Wahlrecht, Recht

    > Questions: 114,115,116

1. Bundestagswahl voters must be citizens of the Federal Republic and at least 18 years old.

    > Keywords: Bundestagswahl, 18, Staatsbürger

    > Questions: 94,108

1. The Bundestag is elected for four years, so Bundestagswahlen normally happen every four years.

    > Keywords: Bundestag, vier Jahre, Bundestagswahl

    > Questions: 107,109,110

1. The Bundestagswahl elects the parliament for Germany.

    > Keywords: Bundestagswahl, Bundestag, Parlament

    > Questions: 124,129

1. The German federal electoral system combines majority and proportional representation.

    > Keywords: Wahlsystem, Mehrheitswahl, Verhältniswahl

    > Questions: 120

1. More Zweitstimmen give a party more parliamentary seats.

    > Keywords: Zweitstimmen, Sitze, Parlament

    > Questions: 93

1. The 5%-Hürde is the minimum vote share to enter parliament and exists because many small parties can make government formation harder.

    > Keywords: 5%-Hürde, Mindestanteil, Regierungsbildung

    > Questions: 117,121,123,127

1. Before an election, eligible voters receive a Wahlbenachrichtigung from the municipality.

    > Keywords: Wahlbenachrichtigung, Gemeinde, Wahl

    > Questions: 126

1. Briefwahl is allowed in Bundestags- and Landtagswahlen.

    > Keywords: Briefwahl, Bundestagswahl, Landtagswahl

    > Questions: 133

1. Wahlhelferinnen and Wahlhelfer count votes after the election and may be a required honorary service for German citizens.

    > Keywords: Wahlhelfer, Ehrenamt, Stimmen zählen

    > Questions: 105,106,282

1. A Landtagswahl is the election of a German state parliament.

    > Keywords: Landtagswahl, Bundesland, Parlament

    > Questions: 62

1. A mayor is the head of a Gemeinde.

    > Keywords: Bürgermeister, Gemeinde, Kommunalpolitik

    > Questions: 131

1. Germany has multiple parties so different citizen opinions are represented.

    > Keywords: Parteien, Demokratie, Meinungen

    > Questions: 41

1. A party can be banned if it fights the constitution or aims to establish a dictatorship.

    > Keywords: Parteiverbot, Verfassung, Diktatur

    > Questions: 20,43

1. A coalition is the cooperation of parties to form a government.

    > Keywords: Koalition, Regierung, Parteien

    > Questions: 31

1. Opposition means the MPs not belonging to the governing party or parties, and it controls the government.

    > Keywords: Opposition, Abgeordnete, Regierungskontrolle

    > Questions: 13,88

1. A Fraktion is the association of a party's MPs in parliament.

    > Keywords: Fraktion, Abgeordnete, Parlament

    > Questions: 89

## Law, Courts & Administration

1. The Bundesverfassungsgericht interprets the Grundgesetz.

    > Keywords: Bundesverfassungsgericht, Grundgesetz, Auslegung

    > Questions: 80

1. A court procedure is called a Prozess, and criminal proceedings happen when someone is accused after committing a crime.

    > Keywords: Prozess, Gericht, Straftat

    > Questions: 139,146

1. Judges work at courts, speak judgments, and Recht sprechen.

    > Keywords: Richter, Urteil, Recht sprechen

    > Questions: 142,147

1. Schöffinnen and Schöffen are honorary judges who decide with professional judges about guilt and punishment.

    > Keywords: Schöffe, ehrenamtlicher Richter, Schuld, Strafe

    > Questions: 140,150

1. Rechtsanwältinnen and Rechtsanwälte advise on legal questions and represent people in court.

    > Keywords: Rechtsanwalt, Rechtsberatung, Gericht

    > Questions: 141

1. The Arbeitsgericht handles workplace conflicts, including unjustified dismissal.

    > Keywords: Arbeitsgericht, Kündigung, Arbeitswelt

    > Questions: 136,137,138

1. Police monitor compliance with laws.

    > Keywords: Polizei, Gesetze, Überwachung

    > Questions: 148

1. Ordnungsamt is an office belonging to municipal administration.

    > Keywords: Ordnungsamt, Gemeindeverwaltung, Verwaltung

    > Questions: 56

1. When moving in Germany, registration is done at the Einwohnermeldeamt.

    > Keywords: Einwohnermeldeamt, Umzug, Anmeldung

    > Questions: 253

1. To protest a wrong tax assessment or wrong official bill, file Einspruch or Widerspruch with the authority.

    > Keywords: Steuerbescheid, Einspruch, Widerspruch, Behörde

    > Questions: 280,283

1. If badly treated by a public authority contact person, complain to the Behördenleiterin or Behördenleiter.

    > Keywords: Behörde, Beschwerde, Behördenleitung

    > Questions: 276

1. Opening a restaurant requires a Gaststättenerlaubnis from the competent authority.

    > Keywords: Gaststättenerlaubnis, Restaurant, Behörde

    > Questions: 256

1. A broken purchased television can be reklamiert.

    > Keywords: Reklamation, Kauf, Verbraucher

    > Questions: 290

## History, Memory & Reunification

1. Hitler became Reichskanzler in 1933, and the National Socialists ruled from 1933 to 1945.

    > Keywords: Hitler, Reichskanzler, Nationalsozialisten, 1933 bis 1945

    > Questions: 152,155,156

1. The NS regime created a dictatorship called the Dritte Reich.

    > Keywords: Drittes Reich, Diktatur, NS-Staat

    > Questions: 157,158

1. Free elections and parties did not exist under National Socialism.

    > Keywords: Nationalsozialismus, freie Wahlen, Parteiverbot

    > Questions: 159,170

1. The NS state was characterized by state racism.

    > Keywords: NS-Staat, Rassismus, Nationalsozialismus

    > Questions: 161

1. The Second World War lasted from 1939 to 1945 and ended in Europe on 8 May 1945.

    > Keywords: Zweiter Weltkrieg, 1939, 1945, 8. Mai

    > Questions: 153,154,160

1. The Second World War in Europe officially ended with Germany's unconditional surrender.

    > Keywords: Kapitulation, Zweiter Weltkrieg, 1945

    > Questions: 179

1. On 9 November 1938, National Socialists and supporters destroyed Jewish shops and synagogues.

    > Keywords: 9. November 1938, Synagogen, jüdische Geschäfte

    > Questions: 163,164

1. Holocaust denial is antisemitic behavior and can be punished with up to five years' imprisonment or a fine.

    > Keywords: Holocaust, Antisemitismus, Freiheitsstrafe, Geldstrafe

    > Questions: 96,149

1. Stolpersteine commemorate the victims of National Socialism.

    > Keywords: Stolpersteine, Opfer, Nationalsozialismus

    > Questions: 206

1. Claus Schenk Graf von Stauffenberg is known for the 20 July 1944 assassination attempt on Hitler.

    > Keywords: Stauffenberg, 20. Juli 1944, Attentat

    > Questions: 162

1. After the Second World War, Germany had four occupation zones run by the USA, Soviet Union, Great Britain, and France.

    > Keywords: Besatzungszonen, Alliierte, USA, Sowjetunion, Großbritannien, Frankreich

    > Questions: 167,175,176

1. Berlin was divided into four sectors after the Second World War.

    > Keywords: Berlin, vier Sektoren, Besatzung

    > Questions: 177

1. The Berlin Airlift supplied West Berlin from June 1948 to May 1949 because the Soviet Union blocked all land routes.

    > Keywords: Luftbrücke, West-Berlin, Sowjetunion, Blockade

    > Questions: 178

1. The Federal Republic of Germany was founded in 1949, with Konrad Adenauer as first Chancellor.

    > Keywords: Bundesrepublik Deutschland, 1949, Konrad Adenauer

    > Questions: 169,165,180

1. The Wirtschaftswunder in West Germany was in the 1950s.

    > Keywords: Wirtschaftswunder, 50er Jahre

    > Questions: 183

1. The DDR was founded in 1949 in the Soviet occupation zone and existed from 1949 to 1990.

    > Keywords: DDR, 1949, 1990, sowjetische Besatzungszone

    > Questions: 172,174,217

1. DDR means Deutsche Demokratische Republik and had a planned economy.

    > Keywords: DDR, Deutsche Demokratische Republik, Planwirtschaft

    > Questions: 190,203

1. The DDR belonged to the Warschauer Pakt in the Cold War.

    > Keywords: DDR, Kalter Krieg, Warschauer Pakt

    > Questions: 202,207

1. The Stasi was the DDR secret service, formally the Ministry for State Security.

    > Keywords: Stasi, DDR, Geheimdienst, Ministerium für Staatssicherheit

    > Questions: 199,208

1. The DDR built the Berlin Wall in 1961, dividing Berlin until it opened in 1989.

    > Keywords: Berliner Mauer, DDR, 1961, 1989

    > Questions: 151,188,189,191,193

1. On 17 June 1953, the DDR saw nationwide strikes and a popular uprising.

    > Keywords: 17. Juni 1953, DDR, Volksaufstand

    > Questions: 186,210

1. In the 1989 Montagsdemonstrationen in the DDR, people shouted Wir sind das Volk.

    > Keywords: Montagsdemonstrationen, Wir sind das Volk, DDR, 1989

    > Questions: 166

1. Die Wende means the DDR changed politically from dictatorship to democracy in autumn 1989.

    > Keywords: Die Wende, DDR, 1989, Demokratie

    > Questions: 196

1. German reunification happened when the five eastern Bundesländer joined the Federal Republic; Helmut Kohl is the Kanzler der Deutschen Einheit.

    > Keywords: Deutsche Wiedervereinigung, neue Bundesländer, Helmut Kohl

    > Questions: 204,215,218,228

    > Keywords: Deutschland, EU, Gründungsmitglied

## Society, Family, Education & Work

    > Keywords: Eltern, 14, Religionsunterricht

    > Keywords: Schulpflicht, Anwesenheitspflicht, Kinder

    > Keywords: Kindergarten, Eltern, Erziehungsberechtigte

1. Children from age three until starting school have a claim to a Kindergarten place.

    > Keywords: Kindergartenplatz, drei Jahre, Ersteinschulung

    > Keywords: Abitur, Universität, Abendgymnasium

1. Volkshochschule is an institution for Weiterbildung.

    > Keywords: Volkshochschule, Weiterbildung

1. Full legal adulthood in Germany begins at 18.

    > Keywords: volljährig, 18

    > Questions: 246

1. Young people are strafmündig from age 14 and can be punished for criminal offenses.

    > Keywords: strafmündig, Jugendliche, 14

1. Child-raising is mainly the parents' responsibility.

    > Keywords: Kindererziehung, Eltern, Familie

    > Questions: 248,249

1. Hitting a child in Germany can be punished, and Jugendamt can remove a child who is beaten or left hungry.

    > Keywords: Kinderschutz, Jugendamt, Strafe

1. For child-raising problems, parents can get help from the Jugendamt.

    > Keywords: Jugendamt, Erziehungsprobleme, Eltern

    > Questions: 255,273

1. Mutterschutz is the special legal protection shortly before and after childbirth.

    > Keywords: Mutterschutz, Schwangerschaft, Geburt

1. To receive Elterngeld, parents must apply at the Elterngeldstelle.

    > Keywords: Elterngeld, Elterngeldstelle, Antrag

    > Questions: 241

1. Couples marry first at the Standesamt, and German law allows marriage to only one partner at a time.

    > Keywords: Standesamt, Ehe, Monogamie

    > Questions: 252,265,272

1. Divorce usually requires the Trennungsjahr and legal support from a lawyer.

    > Keywords: Scheidung, Trennungsjahr, Anwalt

    > Questions: 254,275

1. Adults may choose their own partners; parents must respect a full-age daughter's decision.

    > Keywords: Volljährigkeit, Partnerschaft, Eltern

    > Questions: 267

1. A 13-year-old and a 25-year-old may not live together as a couple.

    > Keywords: Jugendschutz, Paar, 13 Jahre

    > Questions: 245

1. Most employed people in Germany work for a company or public authority.

    > Keywords: Erwerbstätige, Firma, Behörde

1. Good training gives the best chances for a well-paid job, and adults must keep learning after training.

    > Keywords: Ausbildung, Arbeitsplatz, Weiterbildung

    > Questions: 250,284

1. Most social insurance is financed through Sozialabgaben and paid by employers and employees.

    > Keywords: Sozialversicherung, Sozialabgaben, Arbeitgeber, Arbeitnehmer

1. Krankenversicherung and Pflegeversicherung are part of social security or social insurance, but Lebensversicherung is not statutory social insurance.

    > Keywords: Bürgerinitiative, Beteiligung, Buslinie
